title: S Corporation Choice of Entity Election

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must properly elect and maintain S Corporation status to reduce self-employment taxes while paying reasonable compensation and complying with ownership and operational requirements
criteria: Must be a domestic corporation or LLC electing to be taxed as a corporation
Cannot have more than 100 shareholders with only allowable shareholder types (individuals, certain trusts, estates)
Can only have one class of stock (differences in voting rights permitted)
All shareholders must be U.S. citizens or residents (no foreign ownership permitted)
Must file Form 2553 (Election by a Small Business Corporation) timely - within 2 months and 15 days of tax year start
Owner-employees must receive reasonable compensation subject to payroll taxes before taking distributions
Must maintain proper corporate formalities including minutes, resolutions, and separate accounting records
Generally beneficial when business net income exceeds $60,000-$80,000 annually after reasonable salary

s_corp_requirements.self_employment_tax_savings_score: Distributions above reasonable salary avoid 15.3% self-employment tax (12.4% Social Security + 2.9% Medicare)
s_corp_requirements.reasonable_compensation_required: IRS requires market-rate W-2 wages before distributions; penalties for inadequate compensation
s_corp_requirements.payroll_compliance: Must run payroll with proper withholding, quarterly 941 filings, and annual W-2/W-3 reporting
s_corp_requirements.qbi_deduction_interaction: S Corp wages reduce QBI deduction; may need to balance salary vs distribution optimization
s_corp_requirements.state_tax_considerations: Some states impose franchise taxes, fees, or don't recognize S Corp status creating additional costs
s_corp_requirements.late_election_relief: Rev Proc 2013-30 provides relief for late elections if reasonable cause demonstrated

restrictions: Additional compliance costs including payroll processing, payroll taxes, and increased accounting fees
Must pay owner reasonable W-2 salary before taking distributions; IRS heavily audits inadequate compensation
Cannot have partnerships, corporations, or non-resident aliens as shareholders
Limited to one class of stock restricts preferred equity and complex capital structures
Losses flow through to shareholders but limited by stock basis and at-risk amount
Built-in gains tax may apply if converting from C Corporation within recognition period
Fringe benefits for 2%+ shareholders treated less favorably than C Corp shareholders
Termination of S election (inadvertent or voluntary) creates 5-year waiting period before re-election
